step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that the ideal denture base material should not have high specific gravity for several reasons beyond just comfort during insertion and removal. Additionally, materials with lower specific gravity often exhibit better biocompatibility because they tend to be less dense metals like titanium alloys compared to heavier ones such as gold-based alloys. This reduced metal content also means fewer allergens and irritants, further enhancing patient satisfaction by minimizing adverse reactions.
